one_time_description: Insurance proceeds and specific claim negotiation reimbursement of $330,337 received as non-recurring items
economic_notes: Nation holds 8.33% ownership in Prince Albert First Nations Business Development LP and 8.33% in Prince Albert Development Corporation. Received $50,000 distribution from Prince Albert First Nations Business Development LP. Nation is member of Prince Albert Grand Council (12 Nations). Specific treaty benefits claim initiated in 2019 against Government of Canada with pre-settlement trust financing.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020. Bank indebtedness of $301,225 exists (overdraft) with no positive cash balance reported separately on balance sheet. Nation has debt covenant violation on one loan agreement but lender waived right to demand immediate repayment. Two GBEs accounted for under modified equity method: Prince Albert First Nations Business Development LP and Prince Albert Development Corporation (both 8.33% ownership). Consolidated entity includes Shoal Lake Cree Nation Operations and Section 95 Social Housing Operation. One-time items include insurance proceeds ($524,143) and specific claim negotiation reimbursement ($330,337). Total government transfers calculated as ISC + PAGC + FNIHB + Nechapanuk + First Nations Trust + CMHC + NLCDC = 6,860,369 + 1,445,921 + 669,904 + 441,990 + 332,745 + 272,231 + 121,894 = 10,145,054; however expense recovery ($877,388) and deferral adjustment reduce recognized amounts. OSR estimated from rental, other revenue, income from partnerships, admin fees, user fees, insurance proceeds, specific claim reimbursement. No schedule of remuneration provided. Audit report dated January 26, 2022 — significantly late for a March 31, 2020 year-end.
